Life Reflection Math Philosophy Physics

Repost: life update

December 29, 2025

I have not been posting much lately. This was mainly because I had a lot of schoolwork and when the break finally started, I was on a vacation for a little.

I got my usamts score back yesterday, and my distribs were 52402 = 13. I really don't know why I got a 2 when I had almost perfect proof for p5, so I was thinking about appealing, but after I read that my scores might go down as a result of an appeal, my focus is is to do good on r3. I haven't even fully read the questions yet so I really gotta work hard now because I am bascially aiming for hm.

This break is going to be really busy. scioly regionals is coming up right after the winter break, and as one of the leaders, I am like 4% prepared for my events lol. I only do 2 events so it should be fine? but I might really try hard because I want another scioly medal. It would be awesome if our school makes it to states but I don't have much control over that since I only do 2 events, and I am like the only Junior leader, so I have no power.

Robotics competition is coming up soon as well. Our robot isn't even close to being completed, and I still have to finish cadding the bot. CAD takes a bit long for me because I am the only cadder, and I'm not even good at Cading. Also, I like to CAD in every single part of the robot because I feel like our team would just screw it up if I didn't.

the most important thing right now would be studying physics, and I just finished doing odd problems for ch2. I'm like really behind what I planned to do, but it's winter break, so I'm going to try to finish 2 chapters a day. Each chapter, I approximate it to take around 3 hours each, so it should be doable along with other stuff that's going on.

I also started reading and listening to a lot of philosophy. It is really interesting to hear famous philosophers arguing, and I also do learn a lot by just reading some of the famous works. Like who would have expected some random emperor, Aurelius, to be such a cool guy? Now I want to become stoic lol.

Finally, I just wanted to talk about my mental state after missing aime cutoffs by 3 points. So basically what happened was that what I have been building on for past 6 months just collapsed and I have to start over. 6 months of my 2.5 year high school life vanished on something that provided no results. I feel like it's reasonable to be hating this moment. Personally, when I look at the pile of notebook I used for studying or all the books that I used for AMC/aime/usamo grind, I feel so sick. I don't even know why I wasted my time doing AIME and harder oly level questions, when I can't even make aime. I knew it was a gamble when I first started it. I knew that I might end up with nothing, just waste all the time and money away. I don't know why I was so confident on making AIME. Like on July or August, after mocking 90s consistently, I basically thought I was going to get like 120 in November.

But life wouldn't be much fun if everything worked out the first try. I still have an another opportunity to fix this. I hope failing taught me enough, and I don't want to fail again.

Comments
